 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.3","title":"Implement dsr repos command - manage tool registry","description":"# Implement dsr repos command — manage tool registry

## Purpose
Manage the repo registry used by build/release/install. This is the authoritative source for targets, commands, and workflow mappings.

## Registry Layout (XDG)
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/<tool>.yaml` (one file per tool)
- `schema_version: "1.0.0"`
- `repo`, `local_path`, `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets`, `workflow`, `act_job_map`, `checks`
- `artifact_naming`, `archive_format`, `enabled`

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr repos list
 dsr repos add <tool>
 dsr repos remove <tool>
 dsr repos info <tool> [--json]
 dsr repos validate
 dsr repos discover [--apply]
```

## Behavior
- Reads/writes `repos.d/` (merge all tool files deterministically)
- Validates required fields (repo, build_cmd, targets, workflow jobs)
- `discover` scans `/data/projects` for Cargo.toml/go.mod/package.json
- Discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never creates new directories; discovery is **read-only** unless `--apply`
- Never uses global `cd` for git ops; use `git -C <path>`
- Uses `yq` when available for safe edits; otherwise read-only fallback

## Best-Practice Notes
- Normalize paths (absolute or `~`), reject relative
- Use guardrails for any file deletion (`repos remove`)
- stdout for JSON/paths, stderr for human output only

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List shows all tools with language + targets
- [ ] Add/remove works and persists per-tool files
- [ ] Info shows complete build config
- [ ] Validation catches missing fields
- [ ] Discover finds repos without mutating config unless confirmed
- [ ] Registry uses `repos.d` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:02.877692893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:07:51.168912408Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:06:16.702769732Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr repos command with list, add, remove, info, validate, discover, and sync subcommands.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.4","title":"Implement dsr fallback command - trigger local build pipeline","description":"# Implement `dsr fallback` — Orchestrated Build + Release

## Purpose
End-to-end fallback pipeline when GH Actions is throttled: build, verify, release, and report.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr fallback <tool>
 dsr fallback <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr fallback <tool> --build-only
 dsr fallback <tool> --skip-checks
 dsr fallback <tool> --dry-run
 dsr fallback <tool> --resume
```

## Flow (must be deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (build from tag)
2. Run pre-release checks (unless --skip-checks)
3. Acquire lock + create run_id
4. Build all targets (act + native hosts)
5. Generate manifest + checksums + signatures
6. Verify artifacts
7. Create release and upload assets
8. Emit JSON summary + status report

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Works end-to-end without manual intervention
- [ ] Uses manifest for release validation
- [ ] Supports resume after partial failure
- [ ] Clear logs + JSON output (schema-compliant)
- [ ] --dry-run outputs a deterministic plan without side  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.5","title":"Create default repos.d registry with all 16 tools configured","description":"# Create Default repos.d Registry with All 16 Tools Configured

## Purpose
Ship a complete, ready-to-use repository registry so `dsr` can build/release/install the full Dicklesworthstone toolchain without manual setup.

## Why this matters
Most commands (`check`, `build`, `release`, installers) rely on accurate repo metadata. Missing or inconsistent entries cause brittle behavior and user confusion.

## Location
`~/.config/dsr/repos.d/` (created by `dsr config init`)
- One file per tool: `repos.d/<tool>.yaml`
- Each file contains `schema_version` + tool fields

## Required Fields (per tool)
- `repo` (owner/name)
- `local_path` (absolute path, usually under `/data/projects`, but allow overrides)
- `language` (rust|go|bun|node)
- `binary_name`
- `build_cmd` (reproducible, non-interactive)
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (lint/test/typecheck commands)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`
- `enabled` (bool) to allow opt-out

## Guardrails
- Never create new directories under `/data/projects`
- Missing `local_path` → warn, do not mutate
- Use `yq` for safe writes when available; read-only fallback otherwise

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 16 tools configured with full metadata
- [ ] Paths validated (existence check + absolute)
- [ ] Workflows + job mappings verified
- [ ] Artifact naming matches installer expectations
- [ ] Registry lives in `repos.d/` (no monolithic `repos.yaml`)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:14:13.167130536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.294176214Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T18:45:29.293673988Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:33.023057790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.7","title":"Implement auto-tag from version files","description":"# Auto-Tag from Version Files

## Purpose
Automatically create git tags when version files (Cargo.toml, package.json, VERSION, etc.) are updated. This keeps releases consistent across tools and avoids manual tagging errors.

## Version Sources (configurable)
| Language | File | Pattern |
|----------|------|---------|
| Rust | Cargo.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Go | VERSION or version.go | Version = "X.Y.Z" |
| Bun/Node | package.json | "version": "X.Y.Z" |
| Python | pyproject.toml | version = "X.Y.Z" |

## Implementation Notes
- Use repo registry (`repos.d/<tool>.yaml`) to resolve `local_path`
- **No global cd** — use `git -C <repo>`
- Dirty tree detection via exit codes (`git diff-index --quiet HEAD --`)
- Tag existence via `git show-ref --tags -d "refs/tags/vX.Y.Z"`
- Support `--dry-run` and `--non-interactive`

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr version detect <tool>   # Show current version
dsr version tag <tool>      # Create tag if needed
dsr version tag --all       # Tag all repos with new versions
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect version from all supported language files
- [ ] Skip if tag already exists
- [ ] Create annotated tags
- [ ] Push tags to remote
- [ ] All git operations use `git -C` (no global cd)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:28:57.429934130Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:03:38.381821252Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:03:38.381624401Z","close_reason":"Implemented auto-tag from version files: version.sh module + dsr version detect/tag commands","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.309988979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T17:34:54.300293741Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.1.8","title":"Implement dsr config command and configuration management","description":"# dsr Configuration Management (XDG + Schema Validation)

## Purpose
Centralize configuration so every command uses the same sources of truth for repos, hosts, secrets, and defaults. Consistent config prevents drift and makes automation reproducible.

## XDG Layout
- `~/.config/dsr/`
  - `config.yaml` (global defaults + schema_version)
  - `repos.yaml` (tool registry + build matrix)
  - `hosts.yaml` (build hosts + capabilities)
  - `notifications.yaml`
- `~/.cache/dsr/` (API cache, downloads)
- `~/.local/state/dsr/` (logs, state, run history)

## Precedence Rules
1. CLI flags
2. Environment variables (`DSR_*`)
3. Config files
4. Built-in defaults

## repos.yaml Schema (minimum fields)
- `tool`, `repo`, `local_path`
- `language`, `build_cmd`, `binary_name`
- `targets` (os/arch list)
- `checks` (pre-release gates)
- `workflow` + `act_job_map`
- `artifact_naming` + `archive_format`

## hosts.yaml Schema
- host aliases, platform, capabilities, concurrency limits
- ssh options (connect timeout, identity)

## Migration & Versioning
- `schema_version` required
- `dsr config migrate` upgrades older schemas
- Unknown keys rejected unless `--allow-unknown`

## Commands
```bash
dsr config init
 dsr config show [key]
 dsr config validate
 dsr config set KEY=VALUE
 dsr config edit
 dsr config migrate
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Config loads at startup with deterministic precedence
- [ ] Validation catches missing/invalid keys
- [ ] Defaults are sane and documented
- [ ] `--json` outputs resolved config for automation
- [ ] yq-based edits are safe; fallback mode is read-only","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:23:27.935807062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T15:57:12.840449840Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T15:47:07.588658283Z","close_reason":"Implemented dsr config command with init/show/get/set/validate/edit subcommands.  {"id":"bd-1jt.1.9","title":"Implement GitHub API adapter with caching + rate-limit handling","description":"# Implement GitHub API Adapter with Caching + Rate-Limit Handling

- [ ] All errors map to global exit codes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413349290Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T16:13:44.413268548Z","close_reason":"Implemented GitHub API adapter in src/github.sh with: gh_api() for unified API access (gh CLI or curl fallback), ETag caching in ~/.cache/dsr/github/, rate-limit handling with backoff, and helpers for workflow_runs, releases, tags, compare, and asset upload.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.1.9","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:31.418006660Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2","title":"Theme 2: Local Build Infrastructure","description":"# Local Build Infrastructure

## Purpose
Build binaries locally when GH Actions is throttled, using available hardware:
- Linux builds on Threadripper workstation (trj, local)
- macOS builds on Mac mini (mmini)
- Windows builds on Windows laptop (wlap) or cross-compilation fallback

## Build Hosts

### Threadripper Workstation (trj)
- Connection: local
- OS: Ubuntu Linux
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Linux x86_64, Linux ARM64 (via cross), Windows (via cross)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun, Docker/act

### Mac mini (mmini)
- Connection: ssh mmini (via Tailscale)
- OS: macOS
- Arch: ARM64 (Apple Silicon)
- Can build: macOS ARM64 (native), macOS x86_64 (via arch -x86_64)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

### Windows laptop (wlap)
- Connection: ssh wlap (via Tailscale)
- OS: Windows 10/11
- Arch: x86_64
- Can build: Windows x86_64 (native)
- Toolchains needed: Rust, Go, Bun

## Cross-Compilation Strategy
For targets we can't build natively:
- Rust: cargo-zigbuild or cross for Windows/ARM
- Go: GOOS/GOARCH environment variables
- Bun: bun compile with target flag

## Key Commands
```bash
dsr build ntm                       # Build for all targets
dsr build ntm --target linux-amd64  # Specific target
dsr build ntm --host trj            # Force specific build host
```

## Target Matrix (example)

| Tool | Language | Linux x64 | Linux ARM | macOS ARM | macOS x64 | Windows |
|------|----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| ntm  | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| cass | Rust     | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| bv   | Go       | trj       | trj/cross | mmini     | mmini     | wlap    |
| ...  | ...      | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...       | ...     |

## Build Output
Artifacts + logs live under the state directory:
- `~/.local/state/dsr/artifacts/<tool>/<version>/<target>/`
- `~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/` (per-host logs + metadata)

## Dependencies
- SSH access to build hosts (mmini, wlap)
- Toolchains installed on each host
- Docker + act available on trj for Linux workflows
- Sufficient disk space (~5GB per full build)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Host names and mappings align with `hosts.yaml` (trj/mmini/wlap)
- [ ] Artifacts and logs land in state directory with a manifest
- [ ] Cross-compilation paths documented for Rust/Go/Bun
- [ ] Non-interactive builds run without prompts","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:49:46.916624939Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:17.777193563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1jt.2.1","title":"Implement dsr build command - local compilation","description":"# Implement dsr build command — local + remote compilation

## Purpose
Compile a tool locally or on remote build hosts, producing release-ready binaries with a manifest and logs.

## Command Interface
```bash
dsr build <tool>
 dsr build <tool> --target <target>
 dsr build <tool> --host <host>
 dsr build <tool> --version v1.2.3
 dsr build <tool> --output-dir <dir>
 dsr build <tool> --parallel
 dsr build <tool> --resume
```

## Build Flow (deterministic)
1. Resolve repo config + tag/commit (no dirty tree unless --allow-dirty)
2. Acquire lock + create run_id
3. Select host per target (health + capability matrix)
4. Build via act for Linux jobs; native builds for macOS/Windows
5. Collect artifacts to state dir + manifest
6. Emit JSON summary

## Implementation Notes
- Use `git -C <repo>` for all git operations (no global cd)
- Use `ssh <host> "<cmd>"` with explicit error capture
- Use rsync/scp with retries for artifact collection
- Use manifest schema as source of truth for release

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Builds all targets for Rust/Go/Bun tools
- [ ] Artifacts named per manifest contract
- [ ] Logs captured per host
- [ ] Parallel builds do not collide
- [ ] Resume skips completed targets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:08:35.463350565Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:11:50.526013804Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:11:50.525659928Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete in dsr cmd_build() (lines 1360-1638). ## Purpose
Prevent overlapping builds and enable reliable resume/retry by persisting build state per tool/version.

## Key Behaviors
- Acquire lock per tool+version (state dir)
- Create unique run_id + workspace (no overwrites)
- Persist state JSON with per-host status + artifacts
- Resume uses state to skip completed hosts

## Requirements
- Explicit error handling (no `set -e`)
- State updated after every host step
- Locks released on clean exit or timeout
- Stale lock detection (PID + timestamp + TTL)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Concurrent runs are blocked or queued deterministically
- [ ] State file created and updated per host step
- [ ] --resume continues from last known state
- [ ] Logs reference run_id + state path
- [ ] Lock files are atomic and auto-cleaned when stale","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024490854Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T17:40:21.024473411Z","close_reason":"Implemented build workspace isolation with lock/state machine in src/build_state.sh. 16 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T14:36:39.000455353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.4","title":"Implement comprehensive logging infrastructure for dsr","description":"# Comprehensive Logging Infrastructure for dsr

## Purpose
Provide deterministic, structured logs for every action so failures are diagnosable, retries are safe, and audit trails exist for releases.

## Core Principles
- **Structured JSON Lines** for machine parsing
- **stderr** for human logs (colored), **stdout** reserved for JSON output
- **No \`set -e\`**: explicit error handling with captured exit codes
- Stable \`DSR_RUN_ID\` to correlate events across commands
- **Verbosity levels**: error, warn, info, debug (configurable via -v/-q flags)

## File: src/logging.sh
\`\`\`bash
#\!/usr/bin/env bash
# logging.sh - Structured logging for dsr

set -uo pipefail

# Log levels (numeric for comparison)
declare -A LOG_LEVELS=(
  [error]=0
  [warn]=1
  [info]=2
  [debug]=3
)

# Current log level (default: info)
LOG_LEVEL="${DSR_LOG_LEVEL:-info}"
LOG_FILE="${DSR_LOG_FILE:-}"

# Colors (disabled if not TTY or NO_COLOR set)
if [[ -z "${NO_COLOR:-}" && -t 2 ]]; then
  _RED=$'\033[0;31m' _YELLOW=$'\033[0;33m'
  _GREEN=$'\033[0;32m' _BLUE=$'\033[0;34m'
  _GRAY=$'\033[0;90m' _NC=$'\033[0m'
else
  _RED='' _YELLOW='' _GREEN='' _BLUE='' _GRAY='' _NC=''
fi

# Generate run ID if not set
: "${DSR_RUN_ID:="run-$(date +%s)-$$"}"

# Initialize logging
log_init() {
  local state_dir="${DSR_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.local/state/dsr}"
  local log_dir="$state_dir/logs"
  mkdir -p "$log_dir"
  
  # Set log file if not specified
  if [[ -z "$LOG_FILE" ]]; then
    LOG_FILE="$log_dir/dsr-$(date +%Y%m%d).log"
  fi
  
  # Rotate logs older than 30 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +30 -delete 2>/dev/null || true
  
  # Compress logs older than 7 days
  find "$log_dir" -name 'dsr-*.log' -mtime +7 \! -name '*.gz' \
    -exec gzip -q {} \; 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Check if level should be logged
_should_log() {
  local level="$1"
  [[ "${LOG_LEVELS[$level]}" -le "${LOG_LEVELS[$LOG_LEVEL]}" ]]
}

# Core log function
_log() {
  local level="$1" msg="$2"
  shift 2
  local extras="$*"
  
  _should_log "$level" || return 0
  
  local ts
  ts="$(date -Iseconds)"
  
  # Build JSON log entry
  local json="{"
  json+="\"ts\":\"$ts\","
  json+="\"run_id\":\"$DSR_RUN_ID\","
  json+="\"level\":\"$level\","
  json+="\"cmd\":\"${DSR_CURRENT_CMD:-}\","
  json+="\"msg\":\"$msg\""
  
  # Add optional context fields
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_TOOL:-}" ]] && json+=",\"tool\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_TOOL\""
  [[ -n "${DSR_CURRENT_HOST:-}" ]] && json+=",\"host\":\"$DSR_CURRENT_HOST\""
  [[ -n "$extras" ]] && json+=",$extras"
  json+="}"
  
  # Write to log file
  [[ -n "$LOG_FILE" ]] && echo "$json" >> "$LOG_FILE"
  
  # Human-readable stderr output
  local color
  case "$level" in
    error) color="$_RED" ;;
    warn)  color="$_YELLOW" ;;
    info)  color="$_GREEN" ;;
    debug) color="$_GRAY" ;;
  esac
  
  echo "${color}[$level]${_NC} $msg" >&2
}

# Convenience functions
log_error() { _log error "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_warn()  { _log warn "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_info()  { _log info "$1" "${2:-}"; }
log_debug() { _log debug "$1" "${2:-}"; }

# Log with duration tracking
log_timed() {
  local start_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  "$@"
  local exit_code=$?
  local end_ts=$(date +%s%3N)
  local duration_ms=$((end_ts - start_ts))
  
  log_info "Completed: $1" "\"duration_ms\":$duration_ms,\"exit_code\":$exit_code"
  return $exit_code
}

# Export functions
export -f log_init log_error log_warn log_info log_debug log_timed
export DSR_RUN_ID LOG_LEVEL LOG_FILE
\`\`\`

## Log Locations
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/logs/dsr-YYYYMMDD.log\` - Daily session logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/\` - Per-build logs
- \`~/.local/state/dsr/builds/<tool>/<version>/<run_id>/<host>.log\` - Per-host build logs

 {"id":"bd-1jt.5.6","title":"Implement upgrade command verification after release","description":"# Upgrade Command Verification

## Purpose
After releasing binaries, verify that each tool's `upgrade --check` command correctly finds and downloads the new release assets. Catches asset naming mismatches between code and build configs.

## Why This Matters
- Go tools use upgrade.go to detect GOOS/GOARCH and fetch assets
- Rust tools use self-update crates
- Asset naming must match exactly between build and upgrade code
- This verifies the contract end-to-end

## Test Matrix
Run on each platform after release:
| Platform | Command | Expected |
|----------|---------|----------|
| Linux amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-amd64 |
| Linux arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching linux-arm64 |
| macOS arm64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-arm64 |
| macOS amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching darwin-amd64 |
| Windows amd64 | `tool upgrade --check` | Found asset matching windows-amd64 |

## Implementation (No global cd)
```bash
verify_upgrade() {
  local tool="$1"
  local version="$2"
  local repo_dir="/data/projects/$tool"
  local bin_path="$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp/$tool"

  mkdir -p "$repo_dir/.dsr-tmp"

  # Build tool from source first
  if [ -f "$repo_dir/go.mod" ]; then
    go build -o "$bin_path" "$repo_dir/cmd/$tool" 2>/dev/null
  else
    cargo build --release --manifest-path "$repo_dir/Cargo.toml"
    cp "$repo_dir/target/release/$tool" "$bin_path"
  fi

  # Run upgrade check
  output=$("$bin_path" upgrade --check 2>&1)

  if echo "$output" | grep -q "no suitable release asset"; then
    echo "ERROR: Asset naming mismatch for $tool on $(uname -s)/$(uname -m)"
    return 1
  fi

  echo "✓ Upgrade check passed for $tool"
}
```

## dsr Command
```bash
dsr verify upgrade <tool>         # Verify on current platform
dsr verify upgrade <tool> --all   # Verify on all platforms via SSH
```

- [ ] All 16 tools pass upgrade check on all 5 platforms
- [ ] Clear error on asset mismatch
- [ ] Runs automatically after dsr release","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T17:49:59.195937326Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:30:48.638677982Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T13:29:24.668487557Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1jt.5.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1jt.5.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T16:53:14.997083047Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Validate signing command using real minisign when available.

## Approach
- Create temp artifact file.
- If minisign missing, skip with install guidance.
- Run `dsr signing init` in temp XDG config dir.
- Sign and verify the artifact via CLI.

## Logging
- Log key paths and artifact path.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr.

 {"id":"bd-8cg","title":"Update CLI contract + schemas for new commands","description":"# Update CLI Contract + Schemas for New Commands

## Purpose
Keep the CLI contract and JSON schemas authoritative as new commands/features land (status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config migrate, fallback output).

## Scope
- Update `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` with new subcommands, flags, and example JSON payloads.
- Add/extend schema files:
  - `schemas/status-details.json`
  - `schemas/report-details.json` (or reuse status if identical)
  - `schemas/prune-details.json`
  - `schemas/repos-details.json`
  - `schemas/config-details.json`
  - `schemas/fallback-details.json`
- Ensure the envelope schema includes `schema_version` (additive) and stays backward compatible.
- Align error codes and stream-separation guidance for the new commands.

## Design Notes
- Prefer additive schema changes (no breaking removals).
- Keep names consistent (`status` vs `report`) across docs, schemas, and CLI help.
- Include explicit `run_id`, `duration_ms`, `warnings`, and `errors` fields in all new schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CLI_CONTRACT documents status/report, prune, repos validate/discover, config validate/migrate, and fallback outputs.
- [ ] New schema files exist and validate against example fixtures.
- [ ] Schema changes are additive and compatible with existing envelope.
- [ ] Examples include both success and error cases for each new command.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Add fixtures for each new schema and validate with `test_json_schemas.sh` (ajv/jq paths).
